2605.12394.title: Detecting overfitting in Neural Networks during long-horizon grokking using Random Matrix Theory
2605.12394.paper_summary: Training Neural Networks (NNs) without overfitting is difficult; detecting that overfitting is difficult as well. We present a novel Random Matrix Theory method that detects the onset of overfitting in deep learning models without access to train or test data. For each model layer, we randomize each weight matrix element-wise, $\mathbf{W} \to \mathbf{W}_{\mathrm{rand}}$, fit the randomized empirical spectral distribution with a Marchenko-Pastur distribution, and identify large outliers that violate self-averaging. We call these outliers Correlation Traps. During the onset of overfitting, which we call the "anti-grokking" phase in long-horizon grokking, Correlation Traps form and grow in number and scale as test accuracy decreases while train accuracy remains high. Traps may be benign or may harm generalization; we provide an empirical approach to distinguish between them by passing random data through the trained model and evaluating the JS divergence of output logits. Our findings show that anti-grokking is an additional grokking phase with high train accuracy and decreasing test accuracy, structurally distinct from pre-grokking through its Correlation Traps. More broadly, we find that some foundation-scale LLMs exhibit the same Correlation Traps, indicating potentially harmful overfitting.
2605.12394.paper_summary_zh: 訓練神經網絡（NNs）而不過擬合是困難的；檢測過擬合同樣困難。我們提出了一種新穎的隨機矩陣理論方法，能在不接觸訓練或測試數據的情況下檢測深度學習模型中過擬合的開始。對於每個模型層，我們逐元素隨機化每個權重矩陣，$\mathbf{W} \to \mathbf{W}_{\mathrm{rand}}$，將隨機化的經驗譜分佈與Marchenko-Pastur分佈擬合，並識別出違反自平均的巨大異常值。我們稱這些異常值為相關陷阱。在過擬合的開始階段，我們稱之為長期理解中的「反理解」階段，相關陷阱形成並隨著測試準確度下降而數量和規模增長，而訓練準確度仍然保持高水平。陷阱可能是良性的，也可能會損害泛化；我們提供了一種實證方法來區分它們，通過將隨機數據傳遞通過訓練好的模型並評估輸出邏輯的JS散度。我們的研究結果顯示，反理解是一個額外的理解階段，具有高訓練準確度和下降的測試準確度，結構上與通過其相關陷阱的前理解不同。更廣泛地說，我們發現一些基礎規模的LLMs表現出相同的相關陷阱，這表明可能存在有害的過擬合。
2605.12394.author: Hari K. Prakash et.al.
2605.12394.authors: Hari K. Prakash, Charles H Martin
